purpose: minimal public schema for bridge-kind / evidence-kind scoring in bounded-slice tier2/tier3 candidate ranking
goal: separate bridge_kind labels from ranking material
make graph-first vs narrow-fallback competition observable
share the same score profile shape across selected and pruned candidates
support future Rust ranking fixes, Ruby fallback fixes, and selected-vs-pruned witness explanations
non_goals: project-wide closure or proof graph design

scoring_present_when: tier=3 and kind=module_companion_file when the candidate participated in ranking
scoring_omitted_when: reasons that did not go through candidate competition

bridge_kind.role: human-facing bridge family label, not the full ranking basis

evidence_partition.rule: callsite and name/path hints must not be the sole primary basis for a candidate

stability.primary_evidence_kinds: emit [] when scoring is present but no primary evidence kinds were materialized
stability.secondary_evidence_kinds: emit [] when scoring is present but no secondary evidence kinds were materialized

intended_runtime_rules: graph_second_hop wins over narrow_fallback when all later tuple dimensions are equal
return_continuation outranks alias_continuation, which outranks require_relative_continuation, which outranks module_companion_fallback
callsite_position_hint and name_path_hint are tie-break material only
docs, tests, and runtime comparison order must match

g7_3: attach scoring to selected/pruned Rust tier2 candidates
replace bridge_kind+call_line compare with score-profile compare
g7_6: keep Ruby graph-first and fallback lanes distinct
avoid collapsing all Ruby candidates into require_relative_chain
g7_7: derive selected-vs-pruned explanation from scoring tuple and evidence arrays
